block_01: How to write IQ Option platform reviews
block_03: Onboarding articles are a highly effective type of affiliate content. Today, we're sharing a template for writing comprehensive IQ Option platform reviews. Follow it step by step and create truly useful materials!
block_04: A striking title that draws attention and clearly states the topic. 80% of visitors will decide whether or not they will further view the post by title alone! If you're out of ideas, use a
block_06: Introduction that describes the article’s scope, aims and structure, i.e. a walkthrough of the main points of the review.
block_07: Broker overview containing the basic information about IQ Option (depending on the region you work with): the date of establishment, available trading instruments, coverage and geography, awards, etc.
block_08: Trading instruments. Describe them, indicate the number of assets and trading conditions (potential profit, multipliers, etc.).
block_09: Minimum deposit and trading amounts. Indicate that the minimum deposit is $10 (or an equivalent in account currency) and that the minimum investment starts from $1.
block_10: Fees. State that IQ Option doesn’t charge users any transaction fees. You can find the full information on commissions in the Terms and Conditions section.
block_11: IQ Option platform tour. You can start with a Traderoom screenshot indicating the main touchpoints: adding an asset, user panel, trading tools, order settings, account settings, etc. Then, elaborate further on the key features:
block_18: Account types. Describe the Practice and Real accounts functionality. Mention that both account types are automatically available after registration. Also, mention the VIP account for non-EEA traders and the conditions for acquiring the VIP status.
block_19: Showcase the payment methods that traders can use to top up their accounts and withdraw profit in their region.
block_20: List the verification steps and all types of documents required for verifying an account.
block_21: Supported platforms: web, desktop, and mobile. Don’t forget to mention the APK and PWA apps.
block_22: Education: video tutorials, blog, and FAQ.
block_23: Customer support: live chat, email, and phone. Mention that live chat is the quickest one, allowing users to ask questions in their native tongue. Use the phone number for the region you’re writing for.
block_24: Trading community. Show the Leaderboard and mention traders’ sentiment, live deals, and trading chats where users can discuss all-things-trading with the peers.
block_25: Tournaments (only for the non-EEA audience). Tell the users how to participate in the tournaments, what share of the prize pool they can count on, indicate the fees, and mention the Rebuy option.
block_26: Conclusion. Finish the piece with a short wrap-up on the key points of the review.
block_28: Use screenshots to illustrate your points — people prefer visual information to text. Do not sacrifice text to images since the text is crucial for SEO purposes.
block_29: Keep it readable: use simple language, break the text up into paragraphs, and highlight important points.
block_30: Don't use stop words and misleading phrases promising "easy money," "guaranteed profit," etc.
block_32: Adapt the content to your target geo: use the relevant payment methods, contacts, platform language on the screenshots, etc.
